subject: Chemistry
title: Why Ice Floats
description: A quick, practical guide to Why Ice Floats.

hook.text: Almost every substance sinks when it freezes. Water does the opposite—ice floats. This is bizarre.

buildup.text: Water molecules form hydrogen bonds. In liquid water, these bonds constantly break and reform.

discovery.text: When water freezes, hydrogen bonds lock into a rigid hexagonal structure with gaps. Ice is less dense than liquid water.

twist.text: If ice sank, lakes would freeze from the bottom up, killing all aquatic life. Floating ice insulates the water below.

climax.text: This single chemical quirk may be why life exists on Earth. Liquid water survives under floating ice.

punchline.text: Hydrogen bonds make ice float. That accident may have saved all life.

quiz.question: Why does ice float on water?
options: Hydrogen bonds create a rigid structure with gaps, making ice less dense
Air bubbles trapped in ice make it buoyant
Ice contains less hydrogen than liquid water
